title: Temperature Scaling in Large Language Models (LLMs)
description:
In this lecture, we learn about a key technique to help LLM Decoding: Temperature Scaling

We understand the importance of temperature scaling, learn about interpreting high and low temperature values and also learn about why it’s called “temperature”.

We understand the theory, mathematical intuition and also do the coding for the entire implementation. 

The key reference book which this video series very closely follows is Build a Large Language Model from Scratch by Manning Publications. All schematics and their descriptions are borrowed from this incredible book!

This book serves as a comprehensive guide to understanding and building large language models, covering key concepts, techniques, and implementations.

Affiliate links for purchasing the book will be added soon. Stay tuned for updates!

0:00 Traditional next token generation 
3:31 Probabilistic next token generation
4:05 Multinomial probability distribution 
6:36 Predicting next token from multinomial 
14:40 What is temperature scaling?
15:40 Coding the temperature scaling
21:17 Interpreting temperature scaling
22:53 Temperature scaling visualised
24:21 Recap and next steps

Link to code file: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1H6E79zXNsdG_Hn8y2wh6p6mj8o5GGJmD/view?usp=sharing

PyTorch Multinomial Probability Distribution:
https://pytorch.org/docs/stable/generated/torch.multinomial.html

Temperature visualized: 
https://medium.com/@harshit158/softmax-temperature-5492e4007f71
